author: @OldManSparkplug
text: "Free Energy" machines *can* be a useful source of efficient flywheel designs.  A lot of them use that as the source of whatever "magic" rubbish they market.  I got a really decent magnetic bearing design from one back in the day...  The whole machine is inevitably nonsense but some of the bits are salvageable for real things.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I agree, it's always interesting to see what kind of ideas people come up with even if they don't always work!

author: @rickypeacock659
text: 👍am very interested in what comes next. Thank you.

author: @elisabethsambleben7638
text: Wow I am happy I found you

author: @angelusmendez5084
text:
Awesome! 🎉
Free energy! lol That reminds me of that self feeding heron fountain wheel you made a while ago, mindblowing indeed

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Yes - I did like that lol

author: @suehunt622
text: shame really, it would have been great to see you try to make it work as calloway alledgedly made it work.  still be great to see what you do with it though.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it is an interesting mechanism for sure

author: @gptech2444
text: These printers have a huge problem with the bed and the only way to fix it is to spend over $200 on a 8mm funssor bed from aliexpress.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I have been printing non stop on it from when i made this video - I have had no problem - so what is the problem? or are you just sneaking in an ad?

author: @lagunafishing
text: Other than nature, the mechanism in a Grandfather clock is the nearest you'll get to free energy.

author: @johnwynne-qx6br
text: 🤔 what no free energy 😢 😅 looks like the printer is working well 😊  👍

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I know - how disappointing lol - the printer is doing well

author: @Dave_D.
text:
I'd say you're going to try to use the magnetic rotation to try and launch your catapult!  

As far as 'free energy', I believe it is possible, we just don't know how to do it.  We, as humans in general, know very little about the world around us....I find it hard to believe that, with such limited knowledge, the 'laws of physics' we have come up with are absolute.

author: @Bill-n9l7d
text: Cool I like it. Finally I saw one work. Until now my only education on three D printers was the part of the movie"Small Soldiers"  but it showed lasers firing at some slimy looking stuff. So that is hollywood fiction. 😊 But I really like the movie.  I can't grow up only my body grew up😊

replies.author: @mulgerbill
replies.text: That would be resin printing. No doubt Hollywood made it look fancy but it is a thing

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Small Soldiers was a great film!

author: @RJ-2U
text: Always very interesting stuff. Can't wait to see what comes next.

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text:
AH HA more proof of the "Maker mentality" being measurable by the dirty fingernails!!! LOL (someone once remarked on this over an array of people, myself included, showing items we made in a genre and how we all had enough fingernail and they were dirty being the mark of a maker though they joked that you had to let your fingernails grow and put dirt under them to stimulate success in making to which we all laughed. (4:37) and on  XD

also I have a working permanent magnet motor design that works, but its a method no one apparently has thought of sadly involving iron and bismuth (gotta stop thinking of it as a spring but as a liquid and how it "flows" into or repells off of material like iron or bismuth based on ferro and dia magnetism but hey I will put a video on it later on rumble and show how and why it works later. in the middle of a move right now, also it has issues with the low melting point of bismuth and how eddy currents cause heat in metals sadly

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am looking forward to seeing it mate

author: @MickEMaus3000
text: I think V Gates need a little push at every revolution to get over a "hump,"  but I wonder if that could be done by a pendulum in some sort of clock mechanism.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I don't know - but give it a try
